threats.title: Permanent Loss of Bribe Rewards for AVM-Delegated Tokens
threats.hypothesis_description: Bribe rewards for tokens delegated to the Automated Voting Manager (AVM) are sent to the individual AutoVotingEscrow contract instead of the user, permanently locking them.
threats.investigation_rationale: The `getReward` function checks if the owner is the AVM manager to route rewards. However, the NFT is owned by the individual AutoVotingEscrow instance, causing the check to fail and rewards to be sent to the instance, which lacks a withdrawal function.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Incorrect owner resolution: `_owner == avm` fails because `_owner` is the AVE instance, not the manager.

preconditions: User has delegated a veNFT to the AVM
Bribe rewards have accrued for the delegated token

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Reward routing relies on an address comparison that will always fail for AVM tokens.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time a user claims bribe rewards for an AVM-delegated token.

operational_questions: Can the AutoVotingEscrow contract be upgraded to rescue funds?
Is there any alternative path to claim these rewards?
validation_requirements: Verify the owner of an AVM-delegated token
Trace the execution of `getReward` for an AVM token

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: User delegates token to AVM via `enableAutoVoting`
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Bribe rewards accrue for the token's votes
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Rewards are transferred to the AutoVotingEscrow contract and permanently locked
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: None, occurs during normal protocol operation.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete loss of bribe rewards for all AVM users.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires contract upgrades or manual compensation.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: ERC20 transfers to AutoVotingEscrow contract addresses
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Users complaining about missing bribe rewards
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Accumulation of reward tokens in AVE contracts

threats.title: Complete Wipeout of Delegated Voting Power on Token Transfer
threats.hypothesis_description: Transferring a token causes the source delegate to lose all delegations from other users due to a flawed ownership check in the delegation library.
threats.investigation_rationale: `moveTokenDelegates` filters tokens using `ownerOfFn(tId) == srcRep`, which drops all tokens owned by delegators, effectively wiping out the delegate's voting power.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Incorrect ownership check: `if(ownerOfFn(tId) == srcRep)` drops delegated tokens.

preconditions: A user has received delegations from other users
The user transfers, mints, or burns a token

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Delegation checkpointing logic incorrectly assumes delegates own the tokens delegated to them.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time a token is transferred.

operational_questions: Does this affect all types of transfers?
Can the voting power be restored?
validation_requirements: Simulate a transfer from an address with active delegations
Check the resulting checkpoint token array

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify a target delegate with significant voting power
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Send a dust veNFT to the target delegate
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: The target's checkpoint is overwritten, dropping all delegated tokens

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Severe disruption of governance and gauge voting.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires all delegators to re-delegate.

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Any time before a critical vote

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Sudden drops in voting power for active delegates

threats.title: Rebase Rewards Permanently Locked for Expired AVM Tokens via claim_many
threats.hypothesis_description: `claim_many` lacks the AVM ownership resolution present in `claim`, sending rewards for expired AVM locks directly to the AutoVotingEscrow contract where they are permanently locked.
threats.investigation_rationale: Inconsistent implementation between `claim` and `claim_many` leads to reward misrouting for AVM users with expired locks.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Missing AVM check in `claim_many` when lock is expired.

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Direct transfer to `ownerOf` without checking if the owner is an AVM contract.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: When a user's lock expires and rewards are claimed.

operational_questions: Are users incentivized to use `claim_many` over `claim`?
Can an attacker call `claim_many` on behalf of victims?
validation_requirements: Test `claim_many` with an expired AVM token

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify expired AVM-delegated tokens with pending rebase rewards
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Attacker calls `claim_many` passing the victim's token IDs
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Rewards are transferred to the AutoVotingEscrow contract
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Victim permanently loses access to their rebase rewards

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Rebase tokens accumulating in AVE contracts
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Third parties claiming on behalf of others

threats.title: Bribe Rewards Rendered Unclaimable due to Unbounded Epoch Iteration
threats.hypothesis_description: The `earned` function iterates over all epochs since `lastEarn`. With a 30-minute epoch, this quickly exceeds the block gas limit, permanently locking rewards for inactive users.
threats.investigation_rationale: Short epoch durations combined with unbounded loops create a severe DoS vector.
deployment_assumptions: WEEK is set to 1800 seconds (30 minutes) as per BlackTimeLibrary

proof_of_signal.pattern: Unbounded loop over `numEpochs` performing multiple storage reads per iteration.

preconditions: User has not claimed bribe rewards for several days

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: O(N) iteration where N grows by 48 per day.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: After a period of user inactivity.

operational_questions: What is the exact gas cost per iteration?
How many days of inactivity trigger the DoS?

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: User naturally forgets to claim rewards for a week
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Transaction reverts due to out-of-gas error

threats.title: Protocol Insolvency Caused by SMNFT Merge Token Burns
threats.hypothesis_description: Merging a normal lock into an SMNFT burns tokens from the contract without decrementing the `supply` variable, causing fractional reserve insolvency.
threats.investigation_rationale: The contract's token balance will fall below the required amount for normal withdrawals, preventing users from exiting.
preconditions: Users merge normal locks into SMNFT locks

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Token burn without corresponding state update for total supply.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time after sufficient SMNFT merges have occurred.

operational_questions: How many tokens have been burned this way?
Is the contract currently insolvent?
validation_requirements: Compare `supply` variable to actual token balance + `smNFTBalance`

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker or normal users merge normal locks into SMNFTs
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Tokens are burned from the VotingEscrow contract
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Contract token balance drops below the sum of all normal locked amounts
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Users attempting to withdraw expired normal locks face transaction reverts

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Users unable to withdraw their locked tokens.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires team to manually fund the contract.

threats.title: Administrative Role Corruption via removeRole Array Mishandling
threats.hypothesis_description: `removeRole` corrupts users' role arrays by replacing the removed role with a global role string instead of the user's last role.
threats.investigation_rationale: Incorrect array indexing during swap-and-pop leads to unintended role assignments in `_addressToRoles`.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `_addressToRoles[rta[i]][k] = _roles[_roles.length -1];` uses global array instead of user array.

preconditions: Admin calls `removeRole` to delete a role from the system

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Swap-and-pop uses the wrong source array for the replacement element.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires admin to remove a role

operational_questions: Do any off-chain systems rely on `addressToRole`?
Can this cause a double-pop if the swapped role matches?
validation_requirements: Call `removeRole` and check `addressToRole` for affected users

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin legitimately removes a deprecated role
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: The contract replaces the role in users' arrays with the last global role (e.g., 'EPOCH_MANAGER')
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Users now appear to hold 'EPOCH_MANAGER' in their role list
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Off-chain systems or future contracts relying on this list grant unintended privileges

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium - requires manual cleanup of user arrays.

threats.title: Global Voting Power Desync due to Checkpoint Loop Limit
threats.hypothesis_description: The `_checkpoint` loop is capped at 255 iterations. With a 30-minute epoch, 5.3 days of inactivity permanently breaks global voting power tracking.
threats.investigation_rationale: The hardcoded loop limit is incompatible with the short epoch duration, leading to unrecoverable state desync.
deployment_assumptions: WEEK is set to 1800 seconds (30 minutes)

proof_of_signal.pattern: `for (uint i = 0; i < 255; ++i)` loop breaks early if gap > 255 weeks.

preconditions: No interactions with VotingEscrow for > 5.3 days

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Hardcoded iteration limit assumes standard 1-week epochs, but protocol uses 30-minute epochs.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: After a period of low protocol activity.

operational_questions: Is there a keeper bot ensuring frequent checkpoints?
What happens to `totalSupply` after the desync?

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Wait for a period of low activity (> 5.3 days)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: The loop maxes out at 255 iterations, failing to reach the current timestamp
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Global slope and bias are permanently desynchronized, corrupting governance quorums and reward distributions

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete breakdown of voting power accounting.

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Discrepancy between `totalSupply` and sum of individual balances

threats.title: Denial of Service in AVM Total Voting Power Calculation
threats.hypothesis_description: `getTotalVotingPower` iterates over all AVMs and all locks (up to 1024 per AVM), performing binary searches, which will exceed the gas limit.
threats.investigation_rationale: Nested unbounded loops over large arrays make the function unusable, breaking integrations that rely on it.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Nested loop calling `balanceOfNFT` which performs a binary search.

preconditions: Many locks created and delegated to AVM

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: O(N*M) complexity for a view function that might be called on-chain by other contracts.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Once AVM usage reaches a critical threshold.

operational_questions: Is `getTotalVotingPower` called by any state-changing functions?
What is the gas cost per lock?

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Attacker delegates all dust veNFTs to the AVM
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: AVM lock arrays fill up, spawning new AVM instances
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Any on-chain call to `getTotalVotingPower` reverts due to out-of-gas

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Failure of integrations relying on AVM total voting power.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium - requires attacker to withdraw locks or contract upgrade.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Mass delegation of dust veNFTs to AVM

threats.title: Delegation Hijacking via Signature Replay
threats.hypothesis_description: Directly calling `delegate` does not increment the user's nonce, allowing previously signed `delegateBySig` messages to be replayed.
threats.investigation_rationale: Nonces are only incremented in `delegateBySig`, failing to invalidate signatures when state changes via other paths.
preconditions: User later manually calls `delegate` to change their delegation

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Incomplete nonce management across different state-changing paths.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: After a user manually changes their delegation.

operational_questions: Are users actively using `delegateBySig`?
Can this be used to swing close governance votes?
validation_requirements: Sign a delegation, manually delegate, then replay the signature

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker obtains a valid `delegateBySig` signature from a whale
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Whale changes their mind and calls `delegate` directly
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: During a critical vote, attacker broadcasts the old signature
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Whale's delegation is reverted to the attacker, swinging the vote

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low - user can just call `delegate` again.

threats.title: Unrestricted Creation of Permanent SMNFT Locks
threats.hypothesis_description: Any user can create an SMNFT lock, permanently burning tokens for non-decaying, boosted voting power, potentially capturing governance.
threats.investigation_rationale: The documentation implies SMNFTs are a specialized team feature, but the code lacks any access control.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `create_lock` accepts `isSMNFT` from any caller without role checks.

preconditions: Attacker has a large amount of BLACK tokens

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Powerful protocol feature exposed to public without restrictions.

operational_questions: Was this intended to be public?
What is the economic cost vs governance capture benefit?
validation_requirements: Verify any user can create an SMNFT
Calculate the cost to achieve 51% voting power

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Attacker receives permanent, boosted voting power
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Attacker uses this power to dominate gauge emissions and governance

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires hard fork or massive dilution.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Large SMNFT creations by unknown addresses
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Concentration of voting power in SMNFTs
